trigger: Type a name into the 'Your name' input.
Click the real Save button (the primary control, not the decoy label).
bugs.expected: The name is saved and a green 'Saved' confirmation appears in the toast area.
bugs.actual: An uncaught TypeError is thrown (method called on undefined); 'Saved' never appears.
bugs.crash_signature: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'show')

bugs.expected: The toggle flips and the underlying notifications state updates to match the label.
bugs.actual: Only the visual label flips (Off/On); store.notifications never changes — UI/state desync. No crash.

trigger: Leave 'Display name' empty and enter an email without '@'.
bugs.expected: Submission is blocked: Display name is required and Email must contain '@'.
bugs.actual: Validation is skipped; the form proceeds as if valid and shows 'Continued'.

trigger: Navigate to Profile and read the read-only summary.
bugs.expected: The summary shows the name saved on Settings.
bugs.actual: The summary reads a cache that is never updated, so it always renders blank/stale.

bugs.expected: All state is cleared and the app returns to the Settings screen.
bugs.actual: Nothing is cleared and the app routes to the wrong screen (Profile).

trigger: On Settings, locate the Save control by its visible/accessible label 'Save' and click it.
bugs.expected: The primary Save action runs (see BUG-01).
bugs.actual: The obvious 'Save' label sits on a decorative element; the real button has no accessible name. Naive locators hit the decoy, which does nothing.
bugs.log_signature: missing a11y label on primary action
